goal: Rebuild the Ask flow (question.html entry + question-draw.html selection board for both Quick Pull and Story Spread variants) to match the design document. Move topic chip click from auto-navigate to explicit Continue tap, add a non-functional question text-input placeholder, render selection board with Quick/Story differentiation via a single data-attribute, and add a Past·Present·Future legend above the board on Story mode.
done: Rewrote the <main> blocks in question.html, th/question.html, question-draw.html, and th/question-draw.html to mirror the design doc's ScreenAskEntry / ScreenAskBoardQuick / ScreenAskBoardStory components. ENTRY: new .question-shell wrapper; .question-topbar bilingual eyebrow; italic-serif .question-heading ('What's on your mind? / คุณอยากถามอะไร'); .question-shape-picker block with bilingual 'Pick a shape · เลือกรูปแบบ' eyebrow and two .question-shape-card panels (Story / Quick, each with a gradient number thumbnail [3 plum-mid→plum-pale grad / 1 rose→gold grad], bilingual title row, locale-specific note, and an active-state gold check); non-functional .question-input textarea + bilingual hint; .question-topics chip grid (JS injects bilingual pill chips, no emoji); .question-continue-button gold CTA at bottom. DRAW: new .question-draw-shell wrapper carrying data-question-spread attribute (set to 'story' or 'quick' by js/main.js renderQuestionDraw based on URL ?spread= param); .question-draw-topbar with two eyebrow variants (Quick / Story) toggled by attribute; dashed .question-draw-recap panel displaying the selected topic (existing #question-selected-topic-title id preserved for JS hook); .question-draw-title with two pairs of EN+TH titles (Pick one card / เลือกหนึ่งใบ for Quick; Pick three — past, present, future / เลือกสามใบ · อดีต · ปัจจุบัน · อนาคต for Story) toggled by attribute; .question-draw-legend (Past 1 · อดีต / Present 2 · ปัจจุบัน / Future 3 · อนาคต) shown only when data-question-spread='story'; preserved #question-card-board for setupBoard; preserved #question-counter; .question-draw-cta-row with a small ghost #question-reset shuffle button and the .question-draw-button gold CTA holding two spans (Draw · เปิดไพ่ / Reveal spread · เปิดไพ่) toggled by attribute. JS REFACTOR: js/main.js renderQuestion buildTopicCard → buildTopicChip: new chip renders both EN and Thai title (translations.en + translations.th lookups by topic.titleKey, with fallback) so the chip is bilingual regardless of current locale. Click handler no longer navigates — it sets selectedTopic + state.questionTopic, toggles .is-active class on chips, enables the Continue button. New Continue button click handler does the navigation to /question-draw.html with the captured topic + spread URL params. The .question-spread-btn toggle + .question-spread-btn--active class is preserved exactly; the design's chooser cards carry both classes so existing main.js spreadBtns.forEach handler binds to them. renderQuestionDraw: two new lines set data-question-spread on .question-draw-shell from the URL spread param so the title / legend / button-label CSS swap takes effect. Counter logic, board.setupBoard, shuffle, continue navigation to /reading.html — all unchanged. css/question.css rewritten as a 13-section, 416-line stylesheet with 69 --mt-* token references and zero hex literals (initial draft had #f1d9ee + #fadbe1 in the shared page bg; the gradient moved into a new token --mt-bg-gradient-question in css/theme-tokens.css). cp + cap sync ios; md5 canonical == www == ios for all 6 changed files; node --check js/main.js passes.
affects: meowtarot.com/question.html, meowtarot.com/th/question.html — entry page now matches the design Screen 05 layout (italic-serif 'What's on your mind?' heading, dual chooser panels with gradient number thumbnails + bilingual titles + descriptive notes + active gold-check, non-functional question textarea with bilingual hint, pill-style bilingual topic chips, Continue · ต่อไป gold CTA at bottom). UX change: clicking a topic chip no longer navigates immediately. The user must tap Continue to proceed — explicit-tap pattern consistent with feedback_selection_ux_tap_to_confirm memory.

meowtarot.com/question-draw.html, meowtarot.com/th/question-draw.html — selection board now shows the dashed question-recap panel (currently displays the topic name; will display the typed question once the textarea wiring lands), Quick or Story title based on URL spread, a Past·Present·Future bilingual legend above the board on Story mode, the existing 12-slot card grid (visuals from phase-3-redesign.css §2 .card-slot fallback unchanged), counter, shuffle ghost button, and the Quick/Story-aware gold CTA (Draw · เปิดไพ่ / Reveal spread · เปิดไพ่). All existing selection / continue / saveSelectionAndGo logic intact. Story still selects 3, Quick still selects 1 (QUESTION_SELECTION_COUNTS preserved).
notes: Question text input (textarea#question-text-input) is intentionally NOT wired this round per user decision. The placeholder text matches the design ('What's on your mind?' on EN, 'คุณอยากถามอะไร' on TH) and the bilingual hint sits below. The textarea value is not captured on Continue, not persisted to sessionStorage, and not read by the draw page. Filed as a follow-up. The Past/Present/Future position legend is a CSS-only static label above the board — js/main.js setupBoard was NOT refactored to render per-card position pills, per user decision and per the 'preserve selection logic' instruction. The .selection-badge elements that setupBoard renders on selected cards still show '1', '2', '3' numeric badges; users cross-reference those numbers to the legend. /today/ shares data-page='daily' but not data-page='question' or 'question-draw'; no impact on /today/ from this change. The auto-navigate-on-topic-click behavior was removed: previously topic-chip click → trackTopicSelected + window.location.href = /question-draw.html with topic+spread params. Now topic-chip click only sets state.questionTopic + active visual + enables Continue button; navigation moved to Continue button click. trackTopicSelected analytics still fires on topic-chip click (kept the same call site).
verified: md5 canonical == www == ios for all 7 changed files

grep classes per page: .question-shell + .question-shape-picker + .question-shape-card + .question-input + .question-heading + .question-topbar + .question-continue-button on question.html + th/question.html; .question-draw-shell + .question-draw-recap + .question-draw-title + .question-draw-legend + .question-draw-button + data-question-spread on question-draw.html + th/question-draw.html
css/question.css hex literals: zero (initial draft had two hex bg colors that moved into css/theme-tokens.css token --mt-bg-gradient-question)
cap sync ios completed; no plugin errors
local http://localhost:8000/question.html returns the new question-shape-card / question-topic-chip-grid (chip elements injected by JS at runtime) / question-input / question-continue-button markup
local http://localhost:8000/question-draw.html returns data-question-spread on the main element + the new recap / title / legend / draw-button markup
filed: Wire #question-text-input value: capture on Continue tap, persist in sessionStorage 'meowtarot_question_text', read on question-draw.html to fill .question-draw-recap__text, pass to /reading.html via URL param or sessionStorage for display alongside the result.

Move .question-draw-recap__text content from showing topic name to showing the typed question. Currently the existing renderQuestionDraw line sets selectedTopicTitle.textContent to the topic title. After text input wiring, prefer the typed question text and fall back to topic title only if no text was entered.

Locale-aware Quick/Story note translations: design hardcodes English notes ('A fast read for a simple question' / 'A fuller read for something that's been on your mind') — TH file has Thai translations inline. If a third locale lands, move to dict[...] lookups in JS rather than HTML inline.

Renderer for #question-reset (Shuffle · สลับไพ่) reuses the existing main.js boardApi.render() — verify this is the intended UX after layout change (button moved from inside a board-toolbar above the grid to inside the bottom CTA row).

The Story spread title 'Pick three — past, present, future' is intentionally English-only in design — TH spans render 'เลือกสามใบ · อดีต · ปัจจุบัน · อนาคต' as the bilingual partner. Verify rendering on small viewports — the long single-line TH text may overflow on narrow screens.
